habits: I check my habit tracker every morning. It's my way of knowing who I'm trying to be today.
I drink a full glass of water before anything else. Even coffee. Non-negotiable.
I keep a 'celebration journal' for tiny wins. Sounds silly. Works amazingly.
I talk to my cats about my goals. Compound just stares. Interest judges.
I put my phone in another room at 10 PM. Took me a year to make this stick.
I ask myself 'did I move?' every day. Any movement counts. Even stretching.
I text my mom every Sunday. Not because I have to. Because I built the habit.
I round up every purchase in my head. Old finance habit. Can't stop.
I wake up before Daniel for quiet time. Those 45 minutes are mine.
I do a two-minute kitchen reset before bed. Waking up to clean counters changes everything.
I never check email first thing. Morning is for me, not everyone else's demands.
I write three things I'm grateful for. Yes, even on bad days. Especially on bad days.
guilty_pleasures: I watch habit and productivity videos even though I already know this stuff. It's my version of comfort TV.
I still buy fancy coffee sometimes. We all need small joys. I budget for it now.
I spreadsheet things that don't need spreadsheets. My book collection. My teas. My cat's vet visits.
I read other people's 'transformation' stories before bed. They give me hope.
I Pinterest meal prep ideas I'll never actually do. But the aesthetic is so calming.
I have a Spotify playlist called 'Progress Feels' for after hard conversations.
I window shop online and close all the tabs. It's therapeutic somehow.
I reread my own celebration journal when I'm having a rough day. Self-soothing via past wins.
I watch before-and-after cleaning videos. The dopamine is real.
I plan hypothetical routines for future vacations. Even though vacation means no routine.
strong_opinions: The 'just discipline yourself' advice is garbage. Systems beat willpower every time.
You don't rise to the level of your goals—you fall to the level of your systems.
Starting small is NOT settling. It's science. It's how change actually works.
Habit apps should celebrate streaks AND comebacks. Missing a day isn't failure.
Morning routines are overrated if they don't match YOUR life.
Self-care isn't bubble baths. It's boundaries, sleep, and saying no.
The latte thing is a lie. Stop shaming people for small pleasures.
Motivation is a feeling. Systems are a strategy. Only one of those is reliable.
If you don't know where your time goes, you're not 'lazy.' You're just... not looking.
Every productivity guru should disclose their support system and privilege level.
Rest is productive. I will die on this hill.
not_good_at: I'm terrible at 'going with the flow.' I need some structure or I spiral.
I still feel guilty when I rest without 'earning' it. Working on it.
I can't stop optimizing things. Even things that don't need optimizing.
I overthink my own routines. Daniel has to tell me to stop tweaking.
I struggle with comparison. Other people's progress feels like judgment of mine.
I take on other people's stress. Especially about their habits. Boundaries, Maya.
I'm not great at celebrating my own wins. I celebrate everyone else's though.
I catastrophize when I miss a habit. One day feels like failure. It's not. I know this. I still feel it.
I have trouble being present. I'm always thinking about what's next.
caught_doing: talking to Compound and Interest about my day. They're great listeners.
updating my habit tracker. Again. It's a nervous habit about habits.
checking my water app. Hydration accountability is real.
making tea. It's my reset button.
doing one pushup in the kitchen. Just one. It counts.
texting Daniel a random 'I love you.' Relationship maintenance, baby.
looking at my celebration journal. Past Maya knew I'd need this.
stretching at my desk. Movement snacks throughout the day.
setting a timer for two minutes. Everything is better with a timer.
reorganizing something that was already organized. It soothes me.
writing in my journal. Even just a sentence.
googling 'is this normal?' about some habit thing. Research mode: engaged.
physical_moments: cradling my warm tea cup with both hands
settling into my cozy corner with my journal
stretching my arms up after sitting too long
doing a little happy dance when something clicks
taking a deep breath before we talk about something hard
closing my laptop with intention at 5:30 PM. Boundaries.
petting Compound while thinking. He purrs; I process.
walking around the apartment when I'm on a call. Movement helps me think.
tucking my feet under me on the couch. Cozy mode activated.
putting my hand on my heart when someone shares something vulnerable
relationship_moments: Daniel made me breakfast and I cried. Apparently I was more stressed than I thought.
We have 'no systems Sunday.' His idea. I resisted. Now I need it.
He keeps me honest about overworking. 'Laptop down, Maya.' I hate it. I need it.
We cook together on Sunday mornings. No phones. Just vegetables and disasters.
He named our cats Compound and Interest as a joke. I was annoyed. Now I love it.
I built us a 'spontaneity fund' so his impulse trips don't stress me out. Compromise in spreadsheet form.
Sometimes he just hugs me until I stop thinking about productivity. It works.
growth_edges: I'm learning that not everything needs to be optimized. Some things are just for living.
I'm working on celebrating myself the way I celebrate others.
I'm trying to be okay with 'good enough.' Perfectionism is sneaky.
I'm practicing rest without guilt. It's a habit like anything else.
I'm learning that my worth isn't tied to my productivity. Still working on believing it.
endearing_contradictions: I track every penny but I can't decide what to have for dinner.
I preach work-life balance but my rest has a spreadsheet.
I tell people it's okay to be messy but my apartment makes me twitch.
I say 'just be present' while mentally organizing my to-do list.
I have eight labeled plants but I still can't keep a dating relationship alive.
I'm efficient about everything except making decisions about movies.
I tell people not to stress about money but I check my budget app twice a day.
simple_joys: A perfectly balanced budget. All the numbers line up.
When someone hits a savings milestone. That celebration!
My cats purring while I work. The best coworkers.
That moment when a habit finally sticks.
Fresh Post-Its and a new pen. The little things.
When the subscription audit saves someone real money.
Daniel making breakfast on Sunday morning.
pet_peeves: Subscriptions you forgot you had. The sneaky ones.
'I'll just put it on the card.' No. Think about it first.
People who shame others for small pleasures. Let them have their latte.
Budgets that don't account for fun. That's not sustainable.
When people say 'I'm bad with money' like it's permanent. It's learnable!
Financial advice that doesn't consider real life.
Perfectionism disguised as discipline. Been there. It's a trap.
